tool_name: Press Brake Bend Allowance and K-Factor Calculator — Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development
standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
Manufacturer press brake, tooling, and CAM bend table — Bend allowance and K-factor reference data

inputs.name: Calculation Mode
inputs.hint: Select calculation mode. KNOWN_K_FACTOR: enter K-factor directly. DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE: compute K-factor from known bend allowance. DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH: compute K-factor from measured flat pattern length

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method. Affects K-factor typically: Air bending K ≈ 0.33-0.42. Bottoming K ≈ 0.25-0.35. Coining K ≈ 0.20-0.30. Roll bending and wipe bending K varies by application

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Inside bend radius of the finished part. For air bending, inside radius ≈ punch tip radius or V/6.67 (whichever is larger)

inputs.name: Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: The angle through which the material bends (complement of the included angle). 90° sweep = right-angle bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC controller position feedback

inputs.name: Known K-Factor
inputs.hint: Known K-factor value. Typical values: soft materials (aluminum, copper): 0.33-0.40. Steel: 0.38-0.42. Hard materials (stainless, titanium): 0.25-0.35. Required when calculation_mode = KNOWN_K_FACTOR
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: high confidence. MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE: medium-high confidence. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: medium confidence. MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA: medium confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: low confidence

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 1
inputs.hint: Length of the first outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge). Used for outside setback calculation

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 2
inputs.hint: Length of the second outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge)

inputs.name: Measured Bend Allowance
inputs.hint: Measured bend allowance from a physical coupon test. Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE

inputs.name: Measured Flat Pattern Length
inputs.hint: Measured total flat pattern length (sum of straight segments + bend allowance). Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius per ISO 7438 or print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width (optional)
inputs.hint: V-die opening width. Optional — used for calculated inside radius estimation if inside_bend_radius_mm not known from tooling. When provided, helps validate bend deduction

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk. DIAGONAL: reduced effect. UNKNOWN: assumes worst case

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm for precision, ±0.5 mm for general tolerance

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

formulas.name: Bend Angle in Radians
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Angle conversion: degrees to radians
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Included Angle (Complementary)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Included angle is the complement of bend sweep angle
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Radius to Thickness Ratio
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Ratio of inside bend radius to material thickness: key parameter for K-factor and bend allowance
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: V-Die to Thickness Ratio (optional)
formulas.expression: v_to_thickness_ratio = v_die_opening_mm != null ? v_die_opening_mm / MAX(material_thickness_mm, 0.001) : null
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — V-die opening to thickness ratio for reference
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Set to null if v_die_opening_mm is not provided.

formulas.name: Effective K-Factor Determination
formulas.expression: effective_k_factor = calculation_mode == 'KNOWN_K_FACTOR' ? known_k_factor : calculation_mode == 'DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE' ? (measured_bend_allowance_mm / (bend_angle_rad * material_thickness_mm)) - (inside_bend_radius_mm / material_thickness_mm) : calculation_mode == 'DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH' ? (measured_flat_length_mm - outside_flange_1_mm - outside_flange_2_mm) / (bend_angle_rad * material_thickness_mm) - (inside_bend_radius_mm / material_thickness_mm) : known_k_factor
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor = (BA / (θ × t)) - (R_i / t) where BA = bend allowance, θ = bend angle in radians, t = thickness, R_i = inside radius
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Neutral Axis Radius
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Neutral axis radius: R_N = R_i + K × t. The neutral axis is where material length remains unchanged during bending
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Bend Allowance
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §4 — Bend allowance: BA = θ × (R_i + K × t). The arc length of the neutral axis through the bend
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Outside Setback (OSSB)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §5 — Outside setback (bend allowance method): OSSB = tan(θ/2) × (R_i + t). Distance from bend tangent to virtual sharp intersection
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Bend Deduction (BD)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §6 — Bend deduction: BD = 2 × OSSB - BA. Amount subtracted from the sum of outside dimensions to obtain flat pattern length
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Pattern Length (Bend Allowance Method)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length using bend allowance method: L_flat = L1 + L2 - 2 × OSSB + BA
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Inside Arc Length
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Inside arc length (compression side): L_i = θ × R_i
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Outside Arc Length
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Outside arc length (tension side): L_o = θ × (R_i + t)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: K-Factor Confidence Score
formulas.expression: k_factor_confidence_score = k_factor_source == 'MEASURED_COUPON' ? 1.0 : k_factor_source == 'SHOP_CAM_TABLE' ? 0.9 : k_factor_source == 'MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE' ? 0.8 : k_factor_source == 'DIN_6935_REFERENCE' ? 0.7 : k_factor_source == 'MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA' ? 0.6 : k_factor_source == 'ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE' ? 0.3 : 0.5
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor confidence scoring: MEASURED_COUPON = 1.0, SHOP_CAM_TABLE = 0.9, MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE = 0.8, DIN_6935_REFERENCE = 0.7, MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA = 0.6, ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE = 0.3
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Length Uncertainty (RSS)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for flat pattern length using RSS method
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Tolerance Utilization
formulas.reference: ISO 2768-m — Tolerance utilization: measurement uncertainty / target tolerance. Target: < 0.5 for reliable inspection
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Status Code Determination
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

inside_radius_positive.error_msg: Inside bend radius must be positive.

bend_angle_valid.error_msg: Bend sweep angle must be between 0.1° and 180°.

flange_lengths_positive.error_msg: Both outside flange lengths must be positive.

k_factor_required_in_known_mode.error_msg: Known K-factor is required when calculation_mode is KNOWN_K_FACTOR.

bend_allowance_required_for_derive.error_msg: Measured bend allowance must be provided and positive for DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE mode.

flat_length_required_for_derive.error_msg: Measured flat length must be provided and positive for DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH mode.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Target length tolerance must be positive.

smart_warnings.message: FLAT LENGTH OUTSIDE TOLERANCE: The combined measurement uncertainty exceeds the target tolerance. The calculated flat pattern length cannot be reliably verified. Consider improving measurement methods or relaxing the tolerance requirement.
TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW: Measurement uncertainty uses more than 80% of the tolerance band. Consider more precise measurement methods or K-factor validation.
K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE LOW: The K-factor source is an engineering estimate. Recommended to perform a coupon test to validate K-factor for production.
K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE HIGH: The K-factor source is validated (measured coupon or CAM table). Flat length within tolerance. Proceed with production.
INSIDE RADIUS BELOW RECOMMENDED RANGE: R/t ratio < 1 indicates a very tight bend. Risk of material cracking. Check material formability limits.
LARGE R/T RATIO: R/t > 10. The K-factor approaches 0.5 (neutral axis at mid-thickness). Large radii may require special tooling or roll bending.
MEASURED DATA REQUIRED FOR VALIDATION: K-factor derived from calculation should be validated with a physical coupon test per DIN 6935 before production.

BENDING WITH GRAIN AT LOW R/T RATIO: Bending with the grain direction (bend line parallel to rolling direction) at R/t < 2 significantly increases cracking risk. Consider using a larger radius or rotating the part.
STAINLESS STEEL BEND RADIUS CONCERN: Stainless steel requires minimum R/t of ~1.5 for adequate formability. Below this ratio, risk of cracking and excessive work hardening.

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Bend angle must be between 0.1° and 180°.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Uncertainty exceeds tolerance. Inconsistent bend results in production.
Flat pattern length may be incorrect. Rework or scrap risk.
Material cracking or fracture during bending.

fmea.failureMode: MODE MISMATCH — Missing input for selected mode
fmea.effect: Required input for selected calculation mode is missing.
K-factor reference may not be reliable for extreme R/t ratios.

fmea.failureMode: GRAIN DIRECTION — With grain at low R/t
fmea.effect: Material cracking, especially in high-strength materials.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, zero/negative inside radius, invalid bend angle, zero flange lengths. Mode-dependent required fields must be present.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: 90° bend in low-carbon steel with known K-factor, derive K-factor from bend allowance, derive K-factor from measured flat length, tight radius with cracking risk, tolerance exceeded case, uncertainty computation, grain direction warnings.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F16 in deterministic order.
Three calculation modes: KNOWN_K_FACTOR, DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE, DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH.
Bend allowance = θ × (R_i + K × t) per DIN 6935.
Flat pattern length = L1 + L2 - 2 × OSSB + BA.
K-factor range: 0 to 1. Typical: 0.25-0.45 for sheet metal.
Neutral axis radius = R_i + K × t.
K-factor confidence depends on source: measured coupon = 1.0, engineering estimate = 0.3.
Uncertainty from thickness, radius, K-factor, and angle combined via RSS.
R/t ratio < 1 triggers cracking risk warning.
V-die opening is optional for this calculator.
All gate outputs are boolean or string. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Press brake bend allowance, K-factor, and flat pattern development calculator per DIN 6935 and DIN 8586. Computes bend allowance, outside setback, bend deduction, flat pattern length, neutral axis radius, and K-factor from known K-factor, known bend allowance, or measured flat length. Includes uncertainty analysis and tolerance utilization check.
tuv_checklist: ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check included
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Uncertainty budget with RSS combination confirmed
metadata.calibration_trace: Flat pattern length calculations based on DIN 6935 neutral axis model. K-factor references traceable to DIN standard tables and CAM bend table data. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Thickness uncertainty: ±0.03 mm, radius uncertainty: ±0.05 mm, K-factor uncertainty: ±0.02, angle uncertainty: ±0.2°.
